title: Directed Graphs, Strong Components, and Condensation · Cost-Model Clinic: Topological Scheduling
summary: Analyze circular dependencies and group mutually reachable services. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 topological scheduling increment in a strongly-connected dependency report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use DAG order with readiness and parallel-resource constraints without implying arbitrary tasks can overlap.
Return a concrete directed cycle witness for invalid dependency plans.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
